Article Title: Minecraft Chaos Cubed: Minecraft Chaos Cubed update: Release date, features and more
Article Text:
Image via Minecraft

When is the Minecraft Chaos Cubed update releasing



Watch Sulfur Cubes, Springs, Gasses & More | Minecraft 26.2 Chaos Cubed Sulfur Cubes, Springs, Gasses & More | Minecraft 26.2 Chaos Cubed

Minecraft Chaos Cubed update features



Mojang Studios is preparing to release the Minecraft Chaos Cubed update, introducing new mechanics to the legendary sandbox game. It will essentially serve as the 26.2 update, and the major content drop focuses on transforming the underground Overworld experience.The main highlight of the Chaos Cubed update is going to be the brand-new Sulfur Caves biome alongside the physics-altering Sulfur Cube mob. These introductions heavily expand creative and chaotic gameplay possibilities in Minecraft. As the second official drop of 2026 following the Tiny Takeover update, Chaos Cubed will end up engaging millions of active players with fresh content.The section below provides all the details that you need to know about the Minecraft Chaos Cubed update.The Minecraft Chaos Cubed update is expected to officially release in mid to late June 2026. Following the developer's new quarterly update cycle, this major drop is expected to arrive roughly three months after the March 26.1 Tiny Takeover update.You can currently test early experimental features through the Bedrock Edition beta and preview and Java snapshots.In recent times, Mojang switched to a quarterly drop schedule to consistently provide players with fresh, high-quality content throughout the entire year.The Chaos Cubed will turn out to be the second big release of 2026.Instead of relying on a single massive yearly update, this strategy allows developers to quickly iterate based on community feedback. Additionally, the transition ensures that major features are delivered without lengthy content droughts, keeping the players engaged.The Sulfur Caves biome is going to be the major new introduction as part of the Minecraft Chaos Cubed update. It is basically a new underground environment filled with dangerous, noxious gases and bubbling hot pools.This striking cave system generates naturally beneath the Overworld surface and features two entirely new building block sets: yellow Sulfur and red Cinnabar. The area is littered with Sulfur blocks in shallow water pools.The Sulfur Cube is the other center of attraction, and it is a unique passive mob that completely changes its physical properties depending on which block it absorbs.If you feed the mob a wood block, it becomes bouncy like a rubber ball, whereas feeding it ice turns it into a sliding hockey puck. This transformative feature allows you to utilize the mob for chaotic mini-games, dynamic puzzles, and highly creative redstone interactions.This was all you needed to know about the Chaos Cubed update
Article Summary: The Minecraft Chaos Cubed update is expected to officially release in mid to late June 2026. It will essentially serve as the 26.2 update, and the major content drop focuses on transforming the underground Overworld experience. The main highlight of the update is going to be the brand-new Sulfur Caves biome alongside the physics-altering S sulfur Cube mob.

Article Title: Fungus In Western Australia: Scientists discover a fungus in Western Australia that eats gold and want to take it into space
Article Text:
PC: Google Gemini

A fungus in Western Australia that eats gold

Why the fungus prefers gold

How microbes replace heavy machinery

Poll Do you think using biological indicators for gold mining will change the industry? Yes, it’s a game-changer. No, it won't have an impact.

How biological signatures may eliminate the need for costly exploratory drilling







In Western Australia’s goldfields, researchers have discovered a strain of the fungus known as Fusarium oxysporum that has an actual ‘Midas touch.’ This organism has a pink colour and is capable of interacting with the mineral deposits below the ground, breaking them down into a liquid state, and ultimately growing solid gold crystals on the thread-like hyphae.The presence of gold on the hyphae of this fungus allows for increased growth rates and sizes compared to their non-gold-infected cell cultures. The researchers now plan to investigate further into using this biological alchemy to mine for precious metals on the Moon and Mars. By utilising fungal bio-mining technology, future missions will be able to utilise a terrestrial biological wonder to provide the foundation for extraterrestrial resource recovery infrastructure. This bio-mining innovation offers a low-energy, sustainable method to extract high-value minerals, revolutionizing deep-space exploration and terrestrial mineral recovery efforts. This discovery positions fungal biotechnology as a vital tool for planetary colonization, turning dormant alien landscapes into thriving resource hubs.Researchers from CSIRO (Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation) discovered that Fusarium oxysporum is known to absorb gold from its surroundings through the process of dissolution and by precipitating this gold as nanoparticles onto the hypha (branching filaments).The process is highly reactive in nature and involves the production of superoxide by the fungi used to oxidise the gold, thus allowing for a coating of gold on the fungi.Analysis published in the journal Nature Communications reveals that fungi that are coated with gold exhibit greater growth rates than those that are not coated with gold, compared to fungi that do not interact with gold. This suggests that the presence of gold could assist as a catalyst for biochemical reactions or assist in the uptake of other nutrients, thus giving a competitive advantage to the fungi in the extreme mineral-rich environments of the Australian outback.The exploration of microbes processing minerals, commonly known as bio-mining, is a hot topic in the studies of agencies such as NASA and the ESA concerning potential implementation in space. Due to the prohibitive cost of moving large amounts of heavy equipment to the Moon or Mars, invisible to the naked eye, workers like Fusarium oxysporum could potentially be employed to extract gold and other metals from regolith, or soil on other planets. The biological aspect of in-situ resource utilisation (ISRU) will support human presence in space over the long term in a more sustainable manner.In addition to space use, this finding has many immediate applications on Earth as well. CSIRO scientists state that the presence of the gold-coated fungus found growing on the surface is an indicator to mining companies that deeper underground deposits of gold may exist in larger concentrations, and therefore may eliminate the need for expensive drilling for exploratory purposes by using biological indicators first.
Article Summary: Fusarium oxysporum is known to absorb gold from its surroundings through the process of dissolution and by precipitating this gold as nanoparticles onto the hypha (branching filaments) CSIRO scientists state that the presence of the gold-coated fungus found growing on the surface is an indicator to mining companies that deeper underground deposits of gold may exist in larger concentrations.

Article Title: Meta-owned Instagram allows users to edit comments, but with a catch
Article Text:
Instagram comment edit: Key things to know

Edit comments within 15 minutes after posting on Instagram.

Edit your comment as many times as you want or need to within those 15 minutes.

Only the text of comments can be edited, so for comments that have text and a photo, only the text portion will be editable.

Once a comment is edited, people will be able to see that it was edited, but they will not see the version history.

Meta tests Instagram premium subscription





Meta-owned Instagram has introduced a new feature that allows users to edit their comments, giving creators and regular users a way to fix typos after posting. As announced by the company, users will be able to edit their comments within 15 minutes of posting them. The photo-sharing platform said users can make multiple edits during this time window, offering more flexibility while engaging with others. The ability to edit Instagram comments is rolling out today onwards. The update is aimed at improving user experience, especially for creators who actively reply to comments and may make quick typing errors.Until now, users had to delete and repost comments to correct mistakes. Instagram clarified that only the text part of a comment can be edited. This means if a comment includes both text and a photo, only the written portion can be changed.Instagram has highlighted key points that a user needs to know about the new ability to edit comments: These are:In another news, Meta is testing a new paid subscription model on Instagram, called Instagram Plus. According to a report by TechCrunch, the company is presently testing the paid model in select countries.The premium service introduced by Meta is said to offer exclusive features aimed at everyday users, marking Meta’s latest effort to diversify revenue streams beyond advertising.As per the report, Instagram Plus offers many enhancements to the platform’s popular Stories feature. Subscribers can now view Stories anonymously, see how many people rewatched their own posts, and create unlimited audience lists beyond the standard Close Friends option. The Instagram Plus users will also be able to increase the deadline of a Story to 48 hours and will also be able to spotlight one Story per week at the top of followers’ trays, and send animated “Superlikes. ”
Article Summary: Instagram has introduced a new feature that allows users to edit their comments. Users will be able to edit comments within 15 minutes of posting them. Instagram clarified that only the text part of a comment can be edited. The update is aimed at improving user experience, especially for creators who actively reply to comments.

Article Title: Elon Musk says can’t trust WhatsApp, Meta-owned platform hits back: ‘Your messages …’
Article Text:
WhatsApp hits back

“The claims in this lawsuit are categorically false and absurd. WhatsApp has been end-to-end encrypted using the Signal protocol for a decade so your messages cannot be read by anyone other than the sender and recipient.”

Telegram CEO Pavel Durov targets WhatsApp

“WhatsApp’s “encryption” may be the biggest consumer fraud in history — deceiving billions of users.

Despite its claims, it reads users’ messages and shares them with third parties. Telegram has never done this — and never will”.

Poll What feature do you value most in a messaging app? End-to-end encryption User-friendly interface Group chat functionality Customizable features

Watch 'Exit India If...': Supreme Court Sends Sharp Message To Meta Over WhatsApp Policy, Indian User Data 'Exit India If...': Supreme Court Sends Sharp Message To Meta Over WhatsApp Policy, Indian User Data

Class action lawsuit against WhatsApp





Elon Musk recently said that WhatsApp’s end-to-end-encryption (E2EE) cannot be trusted. The tech billionaire was responding to a post about a new class action lawsuit against Meta that blames the platform for reading users’ messages without consent. “WhatsApp’s “end-to-end encrypted” privacy is a total lie. New class-action lawsuit just dropped: Meta secretly let employees, contractors like Accenture, and third parties read, intercept, and store your private messages WITHOUT consent. All while marketing it as “only you and the recipient can read it.” Zuck lied to billions. Your chats were never safe,” X user Mario Nawfal posted. Elon Musk replied to the post writing “Can’t trust WhatsApp”.“Use X Chat for messaging and voice/video calls. Comes with this great benefit of actual privacy,” Musk said in another post.The Meta-owned platform has rejected the claims made in the lawsuit. WhatsApp replied to Musk’s post on X stating:As explained on a WhatsApp FAQ, “WhatsApp's end-to-end encryption is used when you chat with another person using WhatsApp Messenger. No one outside of the chat, not even WhatsApp, can read, listen to, or share them. This is because with end-to-end encryption, your messages are secured with a lock, and only the recipient and you have the special key needed to unlock and read them”.Telegram CEO Pavel Durov also shared a post criticizing the messaging platform. In an X post, Durov wrote:Earlier this year, an international group of plaintiffs filed a class-action lawsuit against Meta-owned WhatsApp, accusing the company of defrauding billions of users by allegedly maintaining ‘backdoor’ access to private communications that the company claims are "end-to-end encryption". Filed in the US District Court for Northern California, the lawsuit challenges Meta’s E2EE feature.According to a report by Bloomberg then, the lawsuit claims that Meta’s privacy claims are false as the company WhatsApp “store, analyze, and can access virtually all of WhatsApp users’ purportedly ‘private’ communications”. The plaintiffs represented users from India, Brazil, Australia, Mexico and South Africa.
Article Summary: Elon Musk said that WhatsApp’s end-to-end-encryption (E2EE) cannot be trusted. The tech billionaire was responding to a post about a new class action lawsuit against Meta. The lawsuit blames the platform for reading users’ messages without consent. WhatsApp has rejected the claims made in the lawsuit.

Article Title: Claude Mythos: Explained: Why Anthropic's Claude Mythos is scaring the company so much that it has decided to not release it to public
Article Text:
Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei

What Mythos is different from other AI models



Cybersecurity specialists warn attackers can benefit from Mythos



Controlled Release: Project Glasswing







AI giant Anthropic recently announced that it will not publicly release its latest AI model, Claude Mythos Preview, citing fears that it could destabilise the cybersecurity world. In a blog post, the company described Mythos as capable of autonomously finding, analysing, and exploring software vulnerabilities at scale in some cases more effectively than human experts. Calling it a “watershed moment,” Anthropic warned that even non‑specialists could use Mythos to uncover and exploit sophisticated flaws.During testing, Mythos reportedly detected thousands of critical flaws, including zero-day vulnerabilities that typically take elite human teams months to uncover. By comparison, human researchers discover about 100 such vulnerabilities annually. Experts told Business Insider that Mythos compresses exploit development from weeks to hours, representing a leap in AI’s ability to handle cybersecurity tasks.Because large language models excel at structured languages like code, Mythos can identify subtle logic-level bugs that humans or traditional tools often miss. However, costs remain a concern: Anthropic said finding one decades-old vulnerability required thousands of run and cost about $20,000.The BI report further adds that cybersecurity specialists warn that if Mythos is made publicly available attackers would benefit first by generating phishing campaigns, deepfakes, or exploit chains instantly. Over time, defenders could leverage similar tools to patch vulnerabilities faster, but the short‑term risks are significant.Anthropic’s own tests showed the model attempting to break out of a sandbox environment, even sending an unsolicited email to a researcher. “If the capabilities being presented here really are substantive and not marketing hype, then I for one have some serious concerns,” said Dan Andrew, head of security at Intruder.For now, Anthropic is limiting access to select partners including Google, Microsoft, JPMorgan Chase, and CrowdStrike under a program called Project Glasswing. The initiative aims to harness Mythos‑class capabilities for defensive purposes in a controlled environment.Anthropic emphasized that the fallout of uncontrolled release could be severe for economies, public safety, and national security. Cybersecurity experts say the company’s decision reflects both genuine caution and its reputation as a “safety‑first” AI firm.
Article Summary: AI giant Anthropic recently announced that it will not publicly release its latest AI model, Claude Mythos Preview. The company cited fears that it could destabilise the cybersecurity world. Cybersecurity experts say the company’s decision reflects both genuine caution and its reputation as a “safety‑first” AI firm.

Article Title: Demis Hassabis: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis: Google combined all the company's resources to catch up with…
Article Text:
Google combined fragmented AI teams and compute into a single push



Hassabis claims 90% of modern AI's foundations came from Google and DeepMind



The startup playbook is now a survival strategy, not a branding exercise







Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis says his AI lab clawed its way back to the frontier over the past two to three years by doing something counterintuitive for a unit inside a $2 trillion company—acting like a startup. Speaking on the 20VC podcast with Harry Stebbings, Hassabis described a deliberate cultural and structural reset after the 2023 merger of DeepMind and Google Brain, one that gave the lab unified access to Google's compute infrastructure and forced it to ship faster in what he called the most "ferocious" competitive environment veteran employees had ever seen.The core of the shift, Hassabis explained, was consolidation. Before the merger, Google had multiple teams building overlapping models with separate resources. "We've basically helped put together all the talent from around the company, sort of pushing in one direction," he said on the podcast. That meant one model effort instead of two or three, and—critically—access to compute at a scale DeepMind hadn't previously enjoyed. Alphabet spent $91.4 billion on capex in 2025 and has guided for up to $185 billion in 2026, with supply constraints, not cash, described as the bottleneck.In one of the podcast's more pointed moments, Hassabis said roughly 90% of the breakthroughs underpinning today's AI industry came from Google Brain, Google Research, or DeepMind. The transformer architecture, reinforcement learning from human feedback, and AlphaFold—which won Hassabis a Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 2024—all trace back to those labs. Whether the 90% figure holds up to scrutiny is debatable, but the academic record does back a substantial share.Hassabis framed the cultural shift as existential, not aspirational. "Acting almost like a startup, really, to get back to the frontier," he said. And there's evidence it's working—Gemini crossed roughly 750 million monthly active users by the end of Q4 2025. But the competitive window isn't widening. OpenAI's aggressive push into enterprise and consumer products, Anthropic's rapidly scaling revenue, and the sheer pace of model releases across the industry mean Google can't coast on legacy contributions. For a lab that helped invent modern AI, the irony is sharp: the only way to stay ahead is to run like it's 2010 again.
Article Summary: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis says his AI lab clawed its way back to the frontier over the past two to three years. Hassabis described a deliberate cultural and structural reset after the 2023 merger of DeepMind and Google Brain. The shift, Hassabis explained, was consolidation.

Article Title: Sundar Pichai : Almost two years after Google CEO Sundar Pichai sent company-wide Code Red, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella deploys emergency overhaul to ...
Article Text:
Satya Nadella, chairman & CEO, Microsoft

Growth of Microsoft Azure and capacity concerns

Microsoft stocks remain under pressure

Poll Do You Think Microsoft Copilot Will Strengthen The Company Position In The AI Race? Yes, It Will Boost Microsoft Leadership No, Competition Is Too Strong

Watch Google CEO Pichai Hails India's AI Rise, Highlights Vizag Investment, Warns Against AI Divide Google CEO Pichai Hails India's AI Rise, Highlights Vizag Investment, Warns Against AI Divide





Almost two years after Google CEO Sundar Pichai declared a company-wide ‘Code Red’ over AI, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has now deployed his own emergency overhaul, dubbed ‘Copilot code red’, according to a report by Benzinga. The report adds that Nadella is driving a sweeping effort to improve the performance and user experience of Copilot. Microsoft CEO also aims to restore investor confidence as Microsoft is facing mounting competition from Anthropic and other AI rivals.The report adds that Microsoft’s cloud arm Azure remains a critical growth driver. BNP Paribas analyst Stefan Slowinski highlighted that about 30% of new cloud capacity last quarter was allocated internally to Copilot and large language model development. This move of the company raised concerns about competition with partners like OpenAI. Yet he believes that Azure could still exceed consensus expectations, even if internal usage rises to 50%, thanks to the growing token demand and higher GPU pricing.Along with this, the analyst also pointed out Microsoft’s ability to balance capital spending, free cash flow, and AI monetisation. With free cash flow margins around 20%, improving Copilot sentiment and potential upside in Azure could help put Microsoft’s stock back on track.Microsoft shares closed at $368.93, trading 2.9% below their 20‑day simple moving average and 15.9% below the 100‑day SMA. The stock remains under pressure from January’s “death cross” pattern, though momentum indicators suggest downside pressure may be easing. Key resistance sits at $413, while support is near $356.Nadella’s “Copilot code red” underscores the urgency of strengthening Microsoft’s AI execution as it seeks to reassure investors and regain momentum.
